               In conclusion, I believe the author is right about how "winning the fight against poverty is achievable in our lifetime". I have faith that phrase will catch on and everyone will pitch in to save the people in poverty.

first_draft:
Based on the article, " A Brighter Future" I believe it is possible to win the fight against poverty because of health care, economy, and the schools.
     First,heath care is one thing that the people could not pay for. The article states that, the plan was to assure the people access to health care. When the village people got sick/ had a disease they would go to the Yala Sub-District Hospital. At the hospital, there were three kids in a bed and two adults in a bed. The rooms were packed with patients who probably would not receive treatment either because the hospital did not have it or the patients could not afford it.           
     Next, the economy was their stability and quality of life. The author states, the next part of the plan was to help them stabilize the economy and quality of life in their communities.Villages got technical advice, and practical items, such as fertilizer, medicine, and school supplies. 
     Last, school is another thing the kids had less of. The kids parents could not afford the fees for school and school supplies.The text says, some kids were needed to help with chores, such as fetching water and wood. In the year 2010, the schools had minimum supplies like books, paper, and pencils, but the students wanted to learn. All of them worked hard with what supplies they had. It was hard for them to concentrate because they had no meal during the day. The students didn't have enough energy to work.
     In conclusion, there is still poverty, but i believe we could work together to win the fight against poverty.
